steps.title: Identify what Text 2 establishes
steps.content: Text 2 states that Bill Monroe built bluegrass by mixing Southern folk music with commercial genres — jazz and blues. This directly contradicts the purists' position that bluegrass should remain rooted purely in folk tradition.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice by rule
steps.content:
Rule: the correct characterization must follow from the specific evidence in Text 2; choices that introduce ideas not grounded in that text are unsupported.
  • Choice A — As inconsistent, since bluegrass purists themselves enjoy other musical genres: Text 2 says nothing about what genres the purists personally enjoy — no support for personal inconsistency. ✗
  • Choice B — As unrealistic, since bluegrass purists have no way of enforcing their musical preferences: Text 2 does not address enforcement power or practicality — unsupported. ✗
  • Choice C — As shortsighted, because bluegrass could enlarge its audience by including influences from mainstream genres: Text 2 makes a historical claim about origins, not a forward-looking argument about audience growth — unsupported. ✗
  • Choice D — As illogical, because the purists overlook crucial aspects of how the bluegrass sound first originated.: Text 2's evidence that Monroe mixed folk with jazz and blues at bluegrass's very origin makes the purists' folk-purity stance logically incoherent. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
